channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Why a Single Retirement Savings Target Doesn’t Fit Every Canadian
item.description: A recent BMO survey found that Canadians believe they need an average of $1.7 million to retire comfortably. Financial experts, however, argue that this figure is not particularly meaningful. Moira Ro...

item.title: Supreme Court Introduces Four-Day Onsite Work Week to Conserve Energy
item.description: Starting March 9, 2026, the Supreme Court of the Philippines will implement a four-day onsite work week with one day of work-from-home (WFH) for its employees. This measure is part of efforts to conse...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Draws on March 8 Yield No Jackpot Winners
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that no one won the jackpot prizes for its major lotto draws held on Sunday night, March 8, 2026. For the Superlotto 6/49, the winning combin...

item.title: FX series Love Story sparks renewed interest in Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy's minimalist fashion
item.description: The FX limited series Love Story, chronicling the romance between John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy, has become the network's most-watched series on streaming platforms, amassing over 2...

item.title: Shantanu Dhakal's Struggle After Nepal's Gen Z Protests
item.description: In September 2025, 18-year-old Shantanu Dhakal was shot in the jaw by police during Nepal's Gen Z protests, sparked by a government ban on major social media platforms. The protests quickly escalated ...

item.title: Lib Dem leader urges cancellation of King's US state visit amid tensions over US-Iran war
item.description: The leader of the Liberal Democrats, Sir Ed Davey, has called on Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer to advise King Charles to cancel his planned state visit to the United States in April. The visit is in...

item.title: Asake Celebrates Mother’s Birthday and Visits Family in Nigeria
item.description: Afrobeats superstar Asake don return to Naija after over one year abroad, and him dey enjoy quality family time. Recently, him show love for him mama by celebrating her birthday for Lagos, even giftin...

item.title: U.S. Temporarily Lifts Russian Oil Sanctions for Indian Purchases Amid Rising Gas Prices
item.description: The Trump administration has defended its decision to temporarily lift certain sanctions on Russian oil, allowing India to continue purchases despite the ongoing conflict in Iran. Officials including ...

item.title: Ukraine's Ambassador in SA Refuses to Sign Condolences for Khamenei Over Drone Supply to Russia
item.description: Ukraine's ambassador to South Africa, Olexander Scherba, has publicly refused an invitation from the Iranian embassy to sign a condolence book following the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Scherba st...

item.title: Cyprus tourism faces early setbacks due to escalating Iran conflict and regional security concerns
item.description: Cyprus, the European Union's closest member to the Middle East and heavily reliant on tourism, is experiencing the initial impacts of the ongoing US-Israeli war against Iran, which began with strikes ...

item.title: GSIS and SSS adopt four-day onsite work week to save energy
item.description: The Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) and the Social Security System (SSS) are implementing a four-day onsite work week starting March 9 as part of the government’s energy conservation effort...

item.title: LAWMA MD Highlights Major Reforms and Infrastructure Upgrades Boosting Waste Collection in Lagos
item.description: The Managing Director of Lagos Waste Management Authority (LAWMA), Dr Muyiwa Gbadegesin, don yarn say big reforms and better infrastructure don seriously improve how waste dey collected for Lagos Stat...

item.title: Canadian Government Begins Evacuation of Citizens from Middle East Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The Canadian government has successfully landed its first chartered flight to evacuate citizens from the Middle East amid escalating regional conflict. The flight, originating from Dubai, had a capaci...

item.title: Brazilian Women Protest Against Rising Gender-Based Violence Following Alleged Copacabana Assault
item.description: In Brazil, International Women’s Day protests highlighted the growing crisis of violence against women, spurred by the alleged gang rape of a 17-year-old in Rio de Janeiro’s Copacabana neighbourhood. ...

item.title: South Africa Advances Regulatory Reforms to Boost Cannabis and Hemp Industry
item.description: South Africa is implementing comprehensive regulatory reforms and commercialisation policies to develop a formal cannabis and hemp industry, aiming to stimulate economic growth, create jobs, and suppo...

item.title: Study Links Receptivity to Corporate Buzzwords with Poorer Decision-Making and Analytical Skills
item.description: A recent study from Cornell University, led by cognitive psychologist Shane Littrell and published in the journal Personality and Individual Differences, explores how susceptibility to corporate bulls...

item.title: Democrat Talarico Faces Major Challenges in Texas Senate Race
item.description: Democratic candidate Talarico has recently gained momentum in Texas, signaling a potential opportunity for the Democratic Party to make inroads in a traditionally Republican stronghold. Despite this p...

item.title: Government launches initiative to empower women sarpanches and curb proxy leadership
item.description: The Government of India has initiated a nationwide social media campaign to tackle the long-standing issue of 'Sarpanch Pati', a practice where male relatives, often husbands, exercise authority in pl...

item.title: Full Schedule and Entry List for Safari Rally Naivasha 2026
item.description: The 2026 Safari Rally, the third round of the World Rally Championships (WRC), is set to kick off in Naivasha from Thursday, March 12, running through Sunday, March 15. This year, the rally will be fu...

item.title: Ben Tudhope secures silver for Australia at 2026 Winter Paralympics
item.description: Australian snowboarder Ben Tudhope claimed silver in the men’s snowboard cross SB-LL2 event at the 2026 Milano Cortina Paralympic Games, marking Australia's first medal of these Games. Despite disloca...
